mentions.note: Highly concentrated growth portfolio—best performer 2020, among worst in 2022; illustrates why undiversified portfolios blow up in multi-year cycles despite exceptional short-term performance in benign environments; concentration is risk masquerading as skill.
Dot-com bust casualty that went to zero; illustrates why buy-and-hold without homework fails in secular downturns; demonstrates false assumption that stocks always recover to prior highs.

Retail victim of Amazon competition; stock cut in half; investors who refused profit-taking due to tax concerns ended up with zero gains; classic example of tax-driven indecision destroying wealth preservation.

FinTech lender exposed to interest rate cycle; business model deteriorated as Fed raised rates; fell $400 (late 2021 peak) to low teens; demonstrates critical distinction between damaged stocks (tactical) and damaged companies (structural).

COVID-era profiteer became damaged company as growth opportunity evaporated post-vaccination; fell $588 (Oct 2020 peak) to $70s within two years; exemplifies why broken business models never recover and catching falling knives fails.
